metadata.llm_reasoning: Frontend depends directly on multiple services; a fault in any of these dependencies can propagate to the symptom frontend. There are no indicated cross-service internal links in the provided topology, so the plausible propagation path is a direct dependency path from any of the listed services to frontend. Given the blast radius includes only frontend, the most plausible root causes are failures in one of its dependencies; no alternative longer chains are evident from the topology. The critical services to monitor are all frontend dependencies, which constitute the blast radius.

result.synthesis_reasoning: The three sources show no reinforcing log evidence and provide limited temporal signals. Topology constrains plausible root causes to frontend dependencies, with productcatalogservice, cartservice, and currencyservice as the most plausible candidates due to their direct dependency relationship with the symptom frontend. The TCB-RCA result centers on frontend itself as the immediate node, but since the symptom is frontend, a deeper upstream root is required to satisfy the unobserved root concept. Given the absence of concrete logs or temporal backtracking data, we assign low confidence across plausible candidates and avoid over-asserting a single root cause. The most credible plan is to monitor the frontend dependencies (productcatalogservice, cartservice, currencyservice) for any rising error signals and examine time-aligned performance metrics to distinguish among them.
